heading: 2022 Releases
body:
> **Important:** On 17 March 2022, MongoDB Atlas moved to Let's Encrypt as the
> new Certificate Authority for TLS (Transport Layer Security) certificates for
> `cloud.mongodb.com`. For more information, see [FAQ: Security](faq-tls-ca).

- Adds the ability to configure some Atlas project limits using the [Projects Administration API resource](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updategroupinvites).

- Adds the ability to push live migrations of replica sets using private endpoints.

- Introduces [Quickly Navigate with Atlas Goto](atlas-go-to).

* Adds the ability to [Simulate Regional Outage](test-outage) for Atlas.

- Introduces [Enable or Disable Termination Protection for the Cluster](scale-cluster-termination-protection)
  for clusters.

- Adds a [maintenance](atlas-modify-project-settings) that lets you
  configure some M40+ clusters with greater maximum storage than the standard limit.

- Adds the Set Oplog Size UI configuration setting. This setting
  allows you to set the [minimum retention window for Oplog entries](set-oplog-min-window).
  You can see the Set Oplog Size configuration setting in the UI only
  if you previously configured it for your cluster. For all new clusters,
  set the Minimum Oplog Window instead.

* Adds three more [Google Cloud Platform (GCP)](google-gcp) regions:

  - `europe-west8` (Milan, Italy)

  - `europe-west9` (Paris, France)

  - `europe-southwest1` (Madrid, Spain)

* Adds the ability to automatically [snapshot copy distribution](snapshot-distribution).

* Improves the [Cluster tier scaling](howitworks-scale-cluster-tier)
  used to auto-scale clusters.

- Introduces the Local NVMe SSD storage option in the Atlas
  UI for some dedicated clusters that run on Azure (Microsoft Azure). Locally
  attached  ephemeral [NVMe storage volumes](nvme-storage) offer the highest
  level of speed and performance. To learn more, see [NVMe storage volumes](nvme-storage).

- Adds the `enableSharding` privilege to custom database roles.

- Adds the ability to set the
  [maximum lifetime](atlas/cluster-additional-settings)
  of multi-document transactions per cluster.

* Supports Azure Private Link for Serverless instances.

* Enhancements to the Atlas billing experience for
  [tax invoices](atlas-international-tax).

- Introduces
  [analytics node tiers](atlas/cluster-config/multi-cloud-distribution).

- Adds support for VPC peering for Prometheus monitoring integration.

- Adds support for VPC peering for [Live Migrate (Push)](atlas/import/migrate-from-com-rs).

- Disallows Atlas clusters on MongoDB 5.0+ from configuring a default
  read concern of `available`.

* Introduces the General Availability of
  MongoDB 6.0.

- Supports [using GitHub credentials](github-accounts) to sign
  in to MongoDB Cloud.

- Adds support for MongoDB 6.0 Release Candidate. Atlas will upgrade
  the cluster to the stable release version when it is generally
  available.

  To learn more about the changes in MongoDB 6.0, see the
  [Release Notes](v6.0/release-notes/6.0).

* Adds additional privileges to custom database roles.

* Adds the `OPLOG_REPLICATION_LAG_TIME` host measurement series to
  the [Measurements Administration API resource](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-returnmeasurementsforonemongodbprocess).

* Updates PagerDuty integration to use the
  [PagerDuty Events API v2](https://developer.pagerduty.com/docs/ZG9jOjExMDI5NTgw-events-api-v2-overview).

- Supports new AWS (Amazon Web Services) [Amazon Web Services (AWS)](amazon-aws): `ap-southeast-3`
  (Jakarta, Indonesia).

- Supports new Google Cloud [Google Cloud Platform (GCP)](google-gcp): `southamerica-west1`
  (Santiago, Chile).

- Supports new Azure (Microsoft Azure) [Microsoft Azure](microsoft-azure):

  - `australiacentral` (Canberra, Australia)

  - `australiacentral2` (Canberra, Australia)

  - `francesouth` (Marseille, France)

  - `norwaywest` (Stavanger, Norway)

  - `swedencentral` (Gävle, Sweden)

  - `swedensouth` (Staffanstorp, Sweden)

  - `southafricawest` (Cape Town, South Africa)

  - `brazilsoutheast` (Rio de Janeiro, Brazil)

  - `westus3` (Arizona, USA)

- Introduces deploying Low-CPU Atlas clusters into additional
  Google Cloud regions:

  - `europe-west3` (Frankfurt, Germany)

  - `europe-west6` (Zurich, Switzerland)

  - `northamerica-northeast1` (Montreal, Canada)

  - `northamerica-northeast2` (Toronto, Canada)

  - `asia-east2` (Hong Kong, China)

  - `asia-northeast2` (Osaka, Japan)

  - `asia-northeast3` (Seoul, South Korea)

  - `asia-southeast2` (Jakarta, Indonesia)

  - `europe-north1` (Finland)

  - `asia-south1` (Mumbai, India)

  - `southamerica-east1` (São Paulo, Brazil)

  - `us-west3` (Salt Lake City, UT, USA)

  - `us-west4` (Las Vegas, NV, USA)

- Spreads newly deployed clusters in the following Azure (Microsoft Azure) regions
  across three [availability zones](microsoft-azure-availability-zones):

  - `brazilsouth` (São Paulo, Brazil)

  - `eastasia` (Hong Kong, China)

  - `norwayeast` (Oslo, Norway)

  - `centralindia` (Pune, India)

  - `koreacentral` (Seoul, South Korea)

- Spreads newly deployed clusters in the following AWS (Amazon Web Services) regions
  across three [availability zones](amazon-aws-availability-zones):

  - `ca-central-1` (Montreal, QC, Canada)

  - `ap-south-1` (Mumbai, India)

  - `ap-northeast-2` (Seoul, South Korea)

  - `sa-east-1` (São Paulo, Brazil)

  - `ap-northeast-1` (Tokyo, Japan)

- Supports [Online Archive](config-online-archive). This feature is in preview.

- Fixes existing behavior where Metrics Chart only shows the duration
  for which data is available.

* Adds support for [upgrading shared tiers](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-upgradegroupsharedcluster) through the Atlas Administration
  API.

* Adds support for [managing project settings](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updateprojectsettings) through the Atlas Administration
  API.

- Introduces a [metrics integration with Prometheus](prometheus-integration).

- Introduces a new [Project Search Index Editor](<Project Search Index Editor>) role to manage MongoDB Search indexes using the Atlas UI or Administration API.

- Introduces the ability to [configure Federated
  Authentication with the Atlas Administration API](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/group/endpoint-federated-authentication).

- Introduces the [Google Cloud Platform (GCP)](google-gcp).

* Upgrades free (`M0`) and shared (`M2` and `M5`) clusters to
  MongoDB 5.0.

* Defaults new clusters to MongoDB 5.0.

- Adds support for the Toronto, Canada (`NORTH_AMERICA_NORTHEAST_2`)
  Google Cloud region.

- Introduces an
  alerts integration with Microsoft Teams.

- Increases the memory for new `M30` to `M200` for Google Cloud
  clusters.

* Adds support for [MongoDB 5.2](manual/../v5.2/reference/versioning).

- Improves the credits table in the Cloud Billing console.

- Changes how the MongoDB Agent rotates `mongosqld` logs to copy and
  truncate.
